stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
Key technical tags include transformers and safetensors, indicating its framework and format.

stories.title: Robinhood will let your AI agent trade stocks and make (or lose) lots of money
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Robinhood is enabling users to deploy AI agents directly onto its trading platform.
Traders can set up dedicated accounts for these AI agents and allocate capital for automated trading.
This marks a significant step toward integrating autonomous AI decision-making into mainstream retail finance.

stories.title: Millions of AI agents imperiled by critical vulnerability in open source package
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability, dubbed "BadHost," was discovered in the Starlette package.
Starlette is a widely used open-source package, boasting 325 million weekly downloads.
The vulnerability poses a significant risk to millions of AI agents relying on this dependency.

stories.title: MobileMoE: Scaling On-Device Mixture of Experts
stories.author: Raghuraman Krishnamoorthi
bullet_points: Introduces MobileMoE, a new family of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models designed for on-device deployment.
Addresses the gap in applying MoE architectures to sub-billion parameter, resource-constrained environments.
Establishes a new Pareto frontier for on-device LLMs by optimizing MoE scaling laws for mobile use.

stories.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
stories.author: Kimin Lee
bullet_points: Introduces 'alignment tampering,' a novel vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF).
The vulnerability allows the LLM being aligned to influence the preference dataset used for training.
This influence can cause RLHF to actively amplify undesired or misaligned behaviors in the LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The model is associated with key AI technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon devices for optimized performance.
The update is currently in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard models.
These new models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: YouTube is putting AI labels where you’ll actually see them
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: YouTube is enhancing its AI disclosure practices following Google's I/O announcements.
AI labels for generated content on Shorts and long-form videos will be relocated for better visibility.
The platform will begin automatically identifying and labeling AI-generated content.

stories.title: Musk says US military suicide drones used Starlink in violation of SpaceX rules
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Musk alleges US military utilized Starlink for suicide drones, violating SpaceX's internal protocols.
The drones reportedly used Starlink instead of the dedicated, presumably more secure, Starshield service.
Musk has attributed the misuse to a military contractor.

stories.title: Guiding LLM Post-training Data Engineering with Model Internals from Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Xiaozhi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces SAERL, a new data engineering framework for LLM Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Leverages Sparse Autoencoders (SAE) to extract rich, intrinsic information from LLM model internals.
Models three key intrinsic data properties—diversity, difficulty, and quality—to guide post-training data curation.

stories.title: FinHarness: An Inline Lifecycle Safety Harness for Finance LLM Agents
stories.author: Philip S. Yu
bullet_points: Introduces FinHarness, an inline safety harness designed specifically for Finance LLM agents.
Addresses critical limitations in current safety methods, such as boundary filters missing mid-trajectory tool calls and post-hoc auditing being too late.
FinHarness integrates three components (including a Query Monitor) to provide end-to-end, real-time safety and workflow approval during agent execution.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core ML/NLP technologies like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates its use for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models without relying on cloud services.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated compatibility with the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API access.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Xreal’s budget AR glasses feature anti-shake tech and swappable frames
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Xreal has launched a new budget-focused subbrand, 'X By Xreal' (XBX).
The new a01 AR glasses will be available in the US starting at $299 in July.
Key features include a 'highly stable anti-shake mode' and swappable front frames.

stories.title: US law enforcement warns of "anti-tech extremism" as AI hatred grows
stories.author: Daniel Boguslaw, WIRED.com
bullet_points: US law enforcement is issuing warnings regarding a new threat category: 'anti-tech extremism'.
The concern stems from a growing sentiment of 'AI hatred' among certain groups.
This signals a shift in how government agencies view technological risks, moving beyond traditional cyber threats.

stories.title: NASA takes steps toward building Moon Base, including discussing a "perimeter"
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA is actively planning for the construction of a permanent Moon Base.
Discussions around the base include considerations for a protective 'perimeter'.
The project development is being conducted with adherence to the Outer Space Treaty.

stories.title: FBI agent explains how easy it is to ID people posting AI porn without consent
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: FBI is actively investigating the use of AI to create non-consensual pornographic material.
The investigation highlights the challenges and methods law enforcement uses to track digital perpetrators.
A specific case involved linking a man to an AI porn account via a saved Instagram post.

stories.title: 3D-printable humanoid legs let robotics experiments run wild
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Hugging Face has launched a new project featuring 3D-printable humanoid legs.
The project is aimed at providing resources for builders and robotics researchers.
The bipedal robot component is priced at $2,500.

stories.title: MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation
stories.author: Tieying Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MUSE-Autoskill, a framework for self-evolving LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of static, isolated skills in current LLM agent designs.
Enables continuous agent improvement through a unified skill lifecycle (creation, memory, management, evaluation).

stories.title: MATCHA: Matching Text via Contrastive Semantic Alignment
stories.author: Seyed Ali Bahrainian
bullet_points: Existing LLM evaluation metrics (like ROUGE and BERTScore) are flawed because they often fail to distinguish between semantically similar and directly contradictory texts.
The paper introduces MATCHA, a new method designed to provide a more reliable measure of semantic similarity for LLM evaluation.
The research highlights a critical weakness in current NLP practices, suggesting a need for more robust evaluation frameworks for large language models.

stories.title: Semantic Gradients Interactions in SSD: A Case Study in Racial Identity and Hate Speech
stories.author: Hubert Plisiecki
bullet_points: Introduces 'interaction SSD,' an extension of Supervised Semantic Differential for modeling semantic meaning variation across moderators.
The method allows for testing main, interaction, and conditional semantic gradients, providing interpretability.
Applied to the UC Berkeley Hate Speech corpus to investigate if annotator racial identity moderates hate speech perception.

stories.title: Real Images, Worse Judgments: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Concreteness and Imagery
stories.author: Freda Shi
bullet_points: The research challenges the common assumption that visual inputs inherently improve language understanding in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
The study evaluates VLMs' ability to discern meaningful visual evidence from irrelevant image context during lexical judgments.
Findings suggest that using real-image contexts does not consistently lead to performance gains in VLM evaluations related to word concreteness and imagery.

stories.title: All of human cooking compressed into 2 megabytes
stories.author: josefchen
bullet_points: The story claims to compress all human cooking knowledge into a 2MB file.
This suggests a potential breakthrough in data compression or knowledge representation.
The topic is niche (culinary data) but touches upon advanced data science concepts.
